turns.content: What tasks are due this week?

📋 SUMMARY: No tasks have specific due dates this week — all 61 open tasks are undated.
🔍 ANALYSIS: None of Jm's 61 incomplete Asana tasks have due dates set. They're organized by project and section (Backlog, Prioritized, In Progress) but without calendar deadlines.

⚡ ACTIONS: Queried Asana for all incomplete tasks and filtered for due dates between Feb 9-15, 2026.

✅ RESULTS: **0 tasks with due dates this week.** However, here are the tasks currently marked **In Progress** that likely need attention:

| Task | Project |
|------|---------|
| **Vday Letter** | Family |
| **Valentine's Day Cooking** | Self |
| **Call Jesse** | Friend |
| **Make magical items in DnD Beyond** | DnD |
| **Create context management system in Kaya** | Professional |
| **Define MVP Value Prop** | Lucidview |

And **Valentine's Day is Saturday Feb 14** — so the Vday Letter and Valentine's Day Cooking are time-sensitive this week.

📊 STATUS: 61 open tasks, 0 with due dates, 6 in progress
📁 CAPTURE: No tasks use due dates — Jm relies on section-based prioritization (Backlog → Prioritized → In Progress)
➡️ NEXT: Consider adding due dates to time-sensitive tasks, especially the Valentine's items

📖 STORY EXPLANATION:
1. Pulled all Asana tasks via CLI to check for this week's deadlines
2. Found 61 incomplete tasks across all projects
3. None have explicit due dates set — all show "no-date"
4. Tasks are organized by section: Backlog, Prioritized, In Progress
5. 6 tasks are currently In Progress, making them the most active
6. Valentine's Day tasks (letter + cooking) are implicitly due this week
7. Other In Progress items: Jesse call, DnD items, Kaya context system, Lucidview MVP
8. Adding due dates would help surface time-sensitive work automatically

⭐ RATE (1-10):

🗣️ Kaya: No dated tasks this week. Valentine's letter and cooking are time-sensitive for Saturday.

tool_calls.name: Bash

params.description: Fetch Asana tasks due this week

params.command: pai-cli asana tasks --json 2>/dev/null | jq '[.[] | select(.due_on != null and .completed == false)] | sort_by(.due_on) | .[] | select(.due_on >= "2026-02-09" and .due_on <= "2026-02-15") | {name, due_on, project: .memberships[0].project.name, section: .memberships[0].section.name}' 2>/dev/null
params.description: Filter tasks with due dates this week (Feb 9-15)

params.command: pai-cli asana tasks --json 2>/dev/null | jq '[.[] | select(.due_on != null and .completed == false and .due_on >= "2026-02-09" and .due_on <= "2026-02-15")]' 2>/dev/null
params.description: Filter tasks due this week with proper jq syntax

params.command: pai-cli asana tasks --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.[] | select(.due_on != null and .completed == false) | "\(.due_on) | \(.name) | \(.memberships[0].project.name)"' 2>/dev/null | sort | head -30
params.description: List all tasks with due dates to find this week's

params.command: pai-cli asana tasks --json 2>/dev/null | jq '[.[] | select(.completed == false)] | length' 2>/dev/null

tool_calls.name: Bash

params.command: pai-cli asana tasks --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.[] | select(.completed == false) | "\(.due_on // "no-date") | \(.name) | \(.memberships[0].project.name // "?") | \(.memberships[0].section.name // "?")"' 2>/dev/null | head -80
params.description: List all incomplete tasks with due dates and projects
